title: acer opalus subsp. granatense
intro: Acer opalus subsp. granatense, also known as the Acer granatum, is a subspecies of the field maple tree, native to the Iberian Peninsula and the western Mediterranean region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine, where it has been used to treat various ailments such as digestive issues and skin problems. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. The herbal preparations of Acer opalus subsp. granatense typically involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
